Here is a story or illustration, taken from customs in the East, about managing sheep. People, as creatures depending on their Creator, are called the sheep of his pasture. The church of God in the world is like a sheep-fold, exposed to deceivers and persecutors. The great Shepherd of the sheep knows all who are his, guards them by his providence, guides them by his Spirit and word, and goes before them, as Eastern shepherds went before their sheep, to set them on the path of his steps. Ministers must serve the sheep in their spiritual needs. The Spirit of Christ will provide an open door for them. The sheep of Christ will observe their Shepherd, and be cautious and wary of strangers who would draw them from faith in him to ideas about him.

Many who hear the word of Christ do not understand it, because they are unwilling to. But we will find one scripture explaining another, and the blessed Spirit making known the blessed Jesus. Christ is the Door. And what greater security has the church of God than that the Lord Jesus is between it and all its enemies? He is a door open for passage and communication. Here are clear directions on how to come into the fold; we must come in by Jesus Christ as the Door, by faith in him as the great Mediator between God and man. Also, we have precious promises to those who follow this direction. Christ has all the care of his church and every believer that a good shepherd has of his flock; and he expects the church and every believer to wait on him and to stay in his pasture.

Christ is a good Shepherd; many were not thieves, yet were careless in their duty, and by their neglect the flock was greatly harmed. Bad principles are the root of bad practices. The Lord Jesus knows whom he has chosen, and is sure of them; they also know whom they have trusted, and are sure of Him. See here the grace of Christ; since no one could demand his life from him, he laid it down himself for our redemption. He offered himself to be the Savior; Lo, I come. And because our situation required it, he offered himself as the Sacrifice. He was both the offerer and the offering, so that his laying down his life was his giving up of himself. From this it is clear that he died in the place and instead of people, to obtain their freedom from the punishment of sin, to obtain the pardon of their sin; and that his death would obtain that pardon. Our Lord laid not his life down for his teaching, but for his sheep.

Satan ruins many by causing them to lose respect for the word and ordinances. People will not be laughed out of their necessary food, yet they allow themselves to be laughed out of what is far more necessary. If our zeal and earnestness in the cause of Christ, especially in the blessed work of bringing his sheep into his fold, brings upon us bad names, let us not pay attention to it, but remember that our Master was reproached like this before us.

All who have anything to say to Christ may find him in the temple. Christ wants us to believe; we make ourselves doubt. The Jews understood his meaning, but could not form his words into a full accusation against him. He described the gracious disposition and happy state of his sheep; they heard and believed his word, followed him as his faithful disciples, and none of them would perish, because the Son and Father are one. Thus he was able to defend his sheep against all their enemies, which proves that he claimed Divine power and perfection equally with the Father.

Christ’s works of power and mercy proclaim him to be over all, God blessed forevermore, so that everyone may know and believe He is in the Father, and the Father in Him. Whoever the Father sends, he sanctifies. The holy God will reward, and therefore will employ, only those whom he makes holy. The Father was in the Son, so that by Divine power he performed his miracles; the Son was so in the Father that he knew the whole of His mind. We cannot fully understand this through searching, but we may know and believe these declarations of Christ.

No weapon formed against our Lord Jesus will succeed. He escaped, not because he was afraid to suffer, but because his time had not come. And He who knew how to deliver himself knows how to deliver the godly out of their temptations, and to make a way for them to escape. Persecutors may drive Christ and his gospel out of their own city or country, but they cannot drive him or it out of the world. When we know Christ by faith in our hearts, we find that all that the Scripture says about him is true.
